perturbation.original_text: For a period of four years from the date of this Agreement (the "Restricted Period"), Seller Parties shall not, directly or indirectly, engage in The Company's Business (as hereinafter defined) or, without the prior written consent of Buyer Parties, directly or indirectly, own an interest in, manage, operate, join, control, lend money or render financial or other assistance to or participate in, as a partner, stockholder, consultant or otherwise, any Person that competes with The Company's Business as it is conducted as of the Closing Date.
perturbation.changed_text: For a period of four years from the date of this Agreement (the "Restricted Period"), Seller Parties shall not, directly or indirectly, engage in The Simtek's Operations (as hereinafter defined) or, without the prior written consent of Buyer Parties, directly or indirectly, own an interest in, manage, operate, join, control, lend money or render financial or other assistance to or participate in, as a partner, stockholder, consultant or otherwise, any Person that competes with The Company's Business as it is conducted as of the Closing Date.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "The Company's Business" to "The Simtek's Operations" creates uncertainty. It is unclear whether 'The Company's Business', as defined later, is equivalent to the 'Simtek's Operations'. This creates an in-text contradiction because it uses two different terms but then continues to refer to 'The Company's Business' creating ambiguity. 'The Company's Business as it is conducted as of the Closing Date' is now contradicing itself as something separate from the Simtek Operations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For a period of four years from the date of this Agreement (the "Restricted Period"), Seller Parties shall not, directly or indirectly, engage in The Simtek's Operations (as hereinafter defined) or, without the prior written consent of Buyer Parties, directly or indirectly, own an interest in, manage, operate, join, control, lend money or render financial or other assistance to or participate in, as a partner, stockholder, consultant or otherwise, any Person that competes with The Company's Business as it is conducted as of the Closing Date. For purposes of this Agreement, the term the "The Company's Business" shall mean any business anywhere in the world that provides products or services of the kind provided by the Business (as defined in the Recitals of the Asset Purchase Agreement) as of the Closing Date. Notwithstanding the foregoing, for the purposes of this Section 1, ownership of securities having no more than one percent (1%) of the outstanding voting power of any competitor which are listed on any national securities exchange, automated securities exchange (including Nasdaq) or traded actively in the national over-the-counter market (in each case including any foreign equivalent) shall not be deemed to be in violation of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'The Company's Business', which is later defined. The changed text replaces this with 'The Simtek's Operations' but then still uses 'The Company's Business' in the same clause. This creates a contradiction because it introduces a second, undefined term ('The Simtek's Operations') while also retaining the original term ('The Company's Business'), potentially creating a conflicting understanding of the obligations. The contradicted text contains the same contradiction because it also uses both terms, creating uncertainty and ambiguity and leading to an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: For the purposes of this Agreement, the term "Confidential Information" shall mean all trade secrets and confidential or proprietary information (and any tangible representation thereof) owned, possessed or used in connection with The Company Business or by the Buyer Parties and its Affiliates; provided, however, that "Confidential Information" does not include information which is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by a Seller Party..
perturbation.changed_text: For the purposes of this Agreement, the term "Restricted Data" shall mean all trade secrets and confidential or proprietary information (and any tangible representation thereof) owned, possessed or used in connection with The Company Business or by the Buyer Parties and its Affiliates; provided, however, that "Confidential Information" does not include information which is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by a Seller Party..
perturbation.explanation: Changing the key term "Confidential Information" to "Restricted Data" while still referencing "Confidential Information" later in the same section introduces a contradiction. It is unclear whether the exclusion of publicly available information applies to 'Restricted Data', leading to ambiguity in what is protected. In addition, the term "Restricted Data" is not a legal term or defined. This creates ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the Restricted Period and at all times thereafter, Seller Parties will keep and hold all Confidential Information (as hereinafter defined) in strict confidence, and will not use or disclose in any way any of such Confidential Information without the prior express written consent of the Buyer Parties. For the purposes of this Agreement, the term "Restricted Data" shall mean all trade secrets and confidential or proprietary information (and any tangible representation thereof) owned, possessed or used in connection with The Company Business or by the Buyer Parties and its Affiliates; provided, however, that "Confidential Information" does not include information which is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by a Seller Party..
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Confidential Information' and the changed text redefines that to 'Restricted Data' while still referring to 'Confidential Information' in the clause 'provided, however, that "Confidential Information" does not include information which is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by a Seller Party..'. The contradicted text uses both terms. The perturbed text creates a contradiction because it redefines a term and then still uses the original term, resulting in two terms ('Restricted Data' and 'Confidential Information') both defining the same or related data. The explanation states 'It is unclear whether the exclusion of publicly available information applies to 'Restricted Data', leading to ambiguity in what is protected.'

perturbation.original_text: During the Restricted Period, Seller Parties agree that they will not in any way, directly or indirectly, for the purpose of conducting or engaging in any business that competes with The Company Business, call upon, solicit, advise or otherwise do, or attempt to do, business with any customers of the Company engaged prior to the Closing Date (provided that Simtek shall have the right to do business with any of its current or former customers), or interfere with or attempt to interfere with any officers, employees or consultants of the Buyer Parties, induce or attempt to induce any of them to leave the employ of the Buyer Parties or violate the terms of their Contracts, or any employment arrangements, with Buyer Parties, or, without the prior written consent of Buyer Parties, directly or indirectly, solicit to hire, hire or employ, whether as an employee, director, contractor, consultant or otherwise any officers, employees or consultants of the Buyer Parties, unless such Person's employment is terminated by the Seller Parties or its Affiliate; provided, however, that this Section 3 shall not prohibit general solicitations of or advertisements for employment by the Seller Parties that are not generally directed at any officers, employees, representatives or agents of the Buyer Parties, so long as the Seller Parties do not hire any such Person who responds to any such general solicitation or advertisement.
perturbation.changed_text: During the Restricted Period, Seller Parties agree that they will not in any way, directly or indirectly, for the purpose of conducting or engaging in any business that competes with The Company's Operations, call upon, solicit, advise or otherwise do, or attempt to do, business with any customers of the Company engaged prior to the Closing Date (provided that Simtek shall have the right to do business with any of its current or former customers), or interfere with or attempt to interfere with any officers, employees or consultants of the Buyer Parties, induce or attempt to induce any of them to leave the employ of the Buyer Parties or violate the terms of their Contracts, or any employment arrangements, with Buyer Parties, or, without the prior written consent of Buyer Parties, directly or indirectly, solicit to hire, hire or employ, whether as an employee, director, contractor, consultant or otherwise any officers, employees or consultants of the Buyer Parties, unless such Person's employment is terminated by the Seller Parties or its Affiliate; provided, however, that this Section 3 shall not prohibit general solicitations of or advertisements for employment by the Seller Parties that are not generally directed at any officers, employees, representatives or agents of the Buyer Parties, so long as the Seller Parties do not hire any such Person who responds to any such general solicitation or advertisement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "The Company Business" to "The Company's Operations" creates ambiguity about the scope of the non-solicitation agreement. It is unclear if 'customers of the Company' are related to 'The Company's Operations,' potentially narrowing the restriction and allowing solicitation of customers related to other aspects of 'The Company Business' not part of "The Company's Operations." This contradiction is within the same section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the Restricted Period, Seller Parties agree that they will not in any way, directly or indirectly, for the purpose of conducting or engaging in any business that competes with The Company's Operations, call upon, solicit, advise or otherwise do, or attempt to do, business with any customers of the Company engaged prior to the Closing Date (provided that Simtek shall have the right to do business with any of its current or former customers), or interfere with or attempt to interfere with any officers, employees or consultants of the Buyer Parties, induce or attempt to induce any of them to leave the employ of the Buyer Parties or violate the terms of their Contracts, or any employment arrangements, with Buyer Parties, or, without the prior written consent of Buyer Parties, directly or indirectly, solicit to hire, hire or employ, whether as an employee, director, contractor, consultant or otherwise any officers, employees or consultants of the Buyer Parties, unless such Person's employment is terminated by the Seller Parties or its Affiliate; provided, however, that this Section 3 shall not prohibit general solicitations of or advertisements for employment by the Seller Parties that are not generally directed at any officers, employees, representatives or agents of the Buyer Parties, so long as the Seller Parties do not hire any such Person who responds to any such general solicitation or advertisement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'The Company Business' to 'The Company's Operations' introduces a contradiction. The original text's use of 'The Company Business' implied a broader scope of prohibited activities regarding customers. By narrowing it to 'The Company's Operations,' the modified text suggests that soliciting customers involved in aspects of the business *outside* of those specific 'Operations' is permissible. This directly contradicts the initial intent to prevent competition across the entirety of 'The Company Business,' creating ambiguity and a potential loophole in the non-solicitation agreement. Because they appear in the same paragraph, this qualifies as an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be amended, modified or supplemented by the parties hereto in any manner, except by an instrument in writing signed by the Seller Parties and the Buyer Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Contract may not be amended, modified or supplemented by the parties hereto in any manner, except by an instrument in writing signed by the Seller Parties and the Buyer Parties.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Agreement" to "Contract" introduces ambiguity. While generally synonymous, within a specific legal context, inconsistent use raises questions. The term "Contract" is not formally defined in this document, while "Agreement" is used to refer to the specific document. It leads to potential disputes over whether amendments to the 'Contract' also apply to the specific 'Agreement' defined earlier, creating a contradiction within the same section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Contract may not be amended, modified or supplemented by the parties hereto in any manner, except by an instrument in writing signed by the Seller Parties and the Buyer Parties.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Agreement" to "Contract" introduces ambiguity as "Contract" is not formally defined, while "Agreement" is used to refer to the specific document. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text: "This Contract may not be amended, modified or supplemented...", which means the change now applies to this sentence too, leading to potential disputes over whether amendments to the 'Contract' (unspecified definition) also apply to the specific 'Agreement' (specified definition) defined earlier. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction', creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Seller Parties recognize that money damages alone would not adequately compensate Buyer Parties and its Affiliates in the event of breach by Seller Parties of this Agreement, and Seller Parties therefore agree that, in addition to all other remedies available to Buyer Parties and its Affiliates, at law, in equity or otherwise, Buyer Parties and its Affiliates shall be entitled to injunctive relief for the enforcement hereof. All rights and remedies hereunder are cumulative and are in addition to and not exclusive of any other rights and remedies available, at law, in equity, by agreement or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: Seller Parties recognize that monetary losses alone would not adequately compensate Buyer Parties and its Affiliates in the event of breach by Seller Parties of this Agreement, and Seller Parties therefore agree that, in addition to all other remedies available to Buyer Parties and its Affiliates, at law, in equity or otherwise, Buyer Parties and its Affiliates shall be entitled to injunctive relief for the enforcement hereof. All rights and remedies hereunder are cumulative and are in addition to and not exclusive of any other rights and remedies available, at law, in equity, by agreement or otherwise.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "money damages" to "monetary losses" introduces subtle ambiguity regarding the scope of compensation. While both refer to financial compensation, 'money damages' typically refers to legally awarded sums, whereas 'monetary losses' is broader and can include lost profits, opportunity costs, etc. It's unclear if the parties intended to limit compensation to legally defined damages or include broader economic losses. This change potentially conflicts with the intent of limiting remedies to 'money damages' while simultaneously referencing more general 'monetary losses.' The contradiction is within the section itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Seller Parties recognize that monetary losses alone would not adequately compensate Buyer Parties and its Affiliates in the event of breach by Seller Parties of this Agreement, and Seller Parties therefore agree that, in addition to all other remedies available to Buyer Parties and its Affiliates, at law, in equity or otherwise, Buyer Parties and its Affiliates shall be entitled to injunctive relief for the enforcement hereof. All rights and remedies hereunder are cumulative and are in addition to and not exclusive of any other rights and remedies available, at law, in equity, by agreement or otherwise.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'money damages' to 'monetary losses' broadens the scope of potential financial remedies. The original text is more specific, relating to legally awarded compensation. The changed text encompasses a wider array of financial losses, including lost profits. This creates an in-text contradiction because the original text suggests a limited form of financial recourse, while the altered text alludes to a more expansive range of potential losses. Even though both texts are located at the same place, the change from 'money damages' to 'monetary losses' generates a contradiction regarding the extent of financial compensation.
